results.title: Making iron
results.creator_credit: John Durst, Film Producers Guild and Merton Park
results.brief_description: Describes with the aid of animated diagrams the construction and operation of the blast furnace. Explains by simple formulae the chemical reactions, showing basic principles of separating iron from ore. The process of smelting and the two-way flow of the burden and hot gases is explained firstly in part, then as a whole, to stress the continuous nature of the operation.

results.title: Making films that teach
results.creator_credit: Hal Kopel and Encyclopaedia Britannica Films
results.brief_description: Explains how scripts for teaching films are prepared and how the films are photographed and produced. Emphasises the value of thorough and careful research in the preparation of educational film strips. The importance of cooperation between education collaborator and producer is also stressed. Illustrates different types of production - historical, dialogue, and location; also special techniques - timelapse photography, animation and x-ray photography. Prepared by the staff of E.B. Films based on 25 years experience in making films that teach.

results.title: Making curriculum relevant
results.brief_description: In this film, young people talk about the perceived irrelevance of school and a teacher explains a work experience program. Also, some teachers discuss what they can do to make the school curriculum relevant to the students they teach, especially in disadvantaged areas where school material often does not reflect the realities of the immediate environment.

results.title: Making cotton clothing
results.brief_description: Traces the production of a child’s cotton print dress, from the design stage to the pressing and packing of hundreds of finished garments ready for despatch to a retail store. Contrasts mass productions techniques in a garment factory to hand-made methods.

results.title: Making coins
results.brief_description: This film is a description of the making of coins in the Royal Mint today, with a brief historical introduction touching on some early British coins. Follows with a detailed tour through the departments of the Royal Mint from the melting house to the final inspection and despatch of the finished coins.

results.title: Making books
results.brief_description: The story of the mass production of books in the USA, showing the ingenuity and technology used at the Country Life Press, Garden City, New York. The film follows every process from the author’s manuscript to the finished book on the bookstall stand.

results.title: Making a Start in Oriental Brush Painting
results.brief_description: An introduction to this beautiful and absorbing art form. Kit Nicol, a talented watercolour artist shows the influence of her long interest in oriental art and culture and that the language of painting is universal. The various sections cover: starting to work, sitting position, brushstrokes, mastering of techniques, painting two subjects (roses and poppies) in full colour, and mounting and framing. Rated: G

results.title: Making a balanced aquarium
results.creator_credit: Coronet Instructional Films and Plymouth Productions
results.brief_description: Two children make a balanced aquarium in a glass tank. They demonstrate the step-by-step procedures of collecting soil and river gravel, placing these and the water in the tank, gathering and planting pond weeds, and then obtaining fish and other water animals to complete their aquarium.
results.description: Two children make a balanced aquarium in a glass tank. results.title: Makin' up!. The Most beautiful breasts in the world
results.creator_credit: Katja Von Garnier, Ewa Karlstrom and Vela-X Filmproduktion
results.brief_description: Makin’ Up! (1992, 54 mins.) is an endearing comedy that focuses on the friendship between two women, Frenzy (Katja Riemann), a cynical, independent cartoonist, and Maischa (Nina Kronjager), a nurse desperately trying to find Mr Right. When Maischa scores a date with a man she has been lusting after, Frenzy is reluctantly forced to entertain his friend for the night. Neither of these interactions turns out as they expected and while both women are forced to re-evaluate their attitudes to romantic love, they realise that while men come and go, their lasting friendship will always provide them with constant pleasure and emotional support. “Makin’ Up!” is peppered with laughs, never taking itself too seriously. It is characterised by strong writing, inventive direction, and a rare chemistry between the two main characters. “The Most Beautiful Breasts in the World” (1990, 15 mins. prods/dirs, Thomas Wobke, Rainer Kaufmann) is a frivolous fantasy about a businessman who unexpectedly finds he has acquired a pair of prominent breasts. Both films in German with English subtitles.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Katja Von Garnier, Ewa Karlstrom and Vela-X Filmproduktion
